text:
Once upon a time, in a quaint village nestled in the green heart of the Welsh mountains, there lived a girl named Gwenda. Gwenda had flaming red hair that danced like firelight in the dimming sunlight. Every year, on the eve of Midsummer Night's Moon, she'd dress in vibrant flowers and go searching for the legendary Fountain of Youth.

This particular Midsummer Eve was special. Gwenda had been searching for years. Her mother had fallen silent as a statue after glimpsing an ancient map that whispered of hidden treasures. Her grandmother told ghostly tales of sparkling water that healed the sick.

So it was that evening when Gwenda stood before an ivy-clad gateway sheltered by whispering oak trees. She fumbled for her small silver lantern, casting beams onto the moss-covered stone.

Her shadow danced behind her. "Come on, ancient waters," she whispered, extending her delicate hand toward the dimpled indentation where sunlight dribbled through leaves.
